title: Neonatal Transfusion
authors: Ravi Mangal Patel & Cassandra D. Josephson

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Neonatal Transfusion?
Blood transfusion เป็น intervention ที่ neonatologist ทำบ่อยที่สุด — 64% ของ ELBW infants (≤1000g) ได้รับ RBC transfusion อย่างน้อย 1 ครั้งใน NICU. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) TOP trial (NEJM 2020) + ETTNO trial (JAMA 2020): higher Hb threshold ไม่ได้ improve NDI — สนับสนุน lower threshold ใน most circumstances; (2) Platelet: PLANET-2 (Curley NEJM 2019): threshold 50 (liberal) เพิ่ม death/severe bleeding vs 25 (restrictive) — ห้ามให้ prophylactic platelet ที่ threshold สูง; (3) Plasma: ไม่มีหลักฐานว่า prophylactic FFP ลด IVH; (4) AS-1/AS-3 units ≥20mL/kg: ห้าม (mannitol → diuresis, adenine → renal toxicity); (5) Delayed cord clamping ลด RBC transfusion + ลด in-hospital mortality — implement ทุก preterm; (6) Cord milking: avoid <28 weeks (Katheria JAMA 2019 — เพิ่ม severe IVH).
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย RBC component preparation: storage solutions (CPDA-1 vs AS-1/AS-3), CMV prevention strategies, storage age evidence
ใช้ตาราง RBC threshold จาก 4 RCTs (Iowa, PINT, TOP, ETTNO) — รู้ liberal vs restrictive Hb values + ผลลัพธ์ของแต่ละ trial
รู้ risks ของ RBC transfusion: immunologic (TRALI, TRAGI) + nonimmunologic (TACO, hypothermia, metabolite derangements)
อธิบาย platelet transfusion: 2 types, dosing, threshold RCTs (Andrew 1993, Curley 2019, Kumar 2019) + PLANET-2 findings
รู้ components ของ plasma + cryoprecipitate: coagulation factors, indications, evidence for prophylactic use
อธิบาย massive transfusion protocol: balanced ratios, whole blood evidence, management principles
อธิบาย exchange transfusion (ET) step-by-step: indications, volumes, procedures, risks (hypocalcemia 29–38%, thrombocytopenia 38–44%)
รู้ IVIG ใน hemolytic disease: mechanism, indications, evidence (Alcock Cochrane 2002 vs Louis 2014)
อธิบาย NAIT: pathophysiology (HPA-1a), incidence, prenatal IVIG, postnatal management
รู้ strategies ลด RBC transfusion: delayed cord clamping vs cord milking, EPO evidence (PENUT trial), minimize phlebotomy
เข้าใจ patient blood management concepts ใน NICU: iron supplementation หลัง conservative transfusion approach
1. Red Blood Cell Transfusion — Components & Preparation
1.1 Storage Solutions และ Volume
CPDA-1: citrate-phosphate-dextrose-adenine; shelf life 35 days at 4°C
AS-1 (Adsol) / AS-3: additive-glucose-mannitol; shelf life 42 days — but important caveat:
AS-1: contains mannitol → osmotic diuresis
AS-3: contains adenine → renal tubular toxicity

⚠️ ห้ามใช้ AS-1 / AS-3 สำหรับ Large-Volume Transfusion (≥20 mL/kg)
AS-1 mannitol → diuresis; AS-3 adenine → renal toxicity. ดังนั้น large-volume (≥20mL/kg) transfusion ต้องใช้ CPDA-1 units. ใน exchange transfusion หรือ massive transfusion: ตรวจสอบ storage solution ก่อนทุกครั้ง. Transfusions should not run >4 hours regardless of solution.
Dose: 20mL/kg vs 10mL/kg — Paul DA JPediatrHematolOncol 2002: 20mL/kg → greater Hct increase without respiratory compromise; optimal dose unclear but common practice
Aliquots จาก parent unit: ลด TACO risk + ลด donor exposure (reuse same unit → ↓ infectious risk)
1.2 CMV Prevention
Most safe: leukoreduced RBCs from CMV-negative donors; TT-CMV risk: 0% (95% CI 0%–0.3%) per unit (Josephson JAMA Pediatr 2014)
Acceptable alternative: leukoreduced from CMV-untested donors — historically up to 1%–3%; likely substantially lower with modern leukoreduction
ห้าม delay transfusion รอแต่ CMV-negative blood ถ้า unavailable — ใช้ CMV-untested แทน
CMV seroprevalence: 40%–80% variation ตาม geographic region (สูงในภาคใต้); ส่งผลต่อ center policy
1.3 Storage Age Evidence
ARIPI trial (Fergusson JAMA 2012;308:1443): VLBW infants → fresh blood (mean 5 days) vs standard (mean 15 days) — ไม่มีความแตกต่างใน composite mortality/morbidity
Concordant findings ใน adults (Lacroix NEJM 2015) และ children (Spinella JAMA 2019)

Note: storage age ≠ accounts for all variability — donor sex/age อาจ matter; observational study (Patel JAMA Netw Open 2021): older female donors → lowest risk of adverse outcomes in VLBW (additional study needed)
1.4 Blood Compatibility ใน Neonates (<4 months)
ABO + D (Rh) = most important blood group systems (>300 antigens total from 35 systems)
สำหรับ infants <4 months: crossmatch + repeated ABO/D typing อาจ omit ถ้าครบทุกข้อ:
1. Antibody screen negative
2. RBCs are group O, ABO identical, หรือ ABO compatible
3. RBCs D negative หรือ same D type as patient
Anti-A/anti-B IgM/IgG: ปกติไม่พัฒนาจนกว่า >4 months → ABO hemolytic reactions less likely in neonates
IgG antibody (maternal): ต้องใช้ crossmatched ABO-compatible RBCs จนกว่า acquired antibody จะ no longer detected
2. Indications for RBC Transfusion — Thresholds
2.1 Epidemiology
64% ของ ELBW infants (≤1000g) ได้รับ RBC transfusion อย่างน้อย 1 ครั้ง (Patel J Pediatr 2021)
Extremely preterm = หนึ่งใน most highly transfused populations in medicine
Wide variation ใน pretransfusion Hb thresholds among US centers
2.2 สรุป 4 RCTs เปรียบ Liberal vs Conservative
ตาราง threshold จาก Avery Table 70.1 (ค่าเป็น g/dL — ช่วงขึ้นกับ respiratory illness severity + postnatal age):
Iowa trial (Bell Pediatrics 2005): RCT liberal vs restrictive; underpowered; nonsignificant difference in outcomes

PINT trial (Kirpalani J Pediatr 2006): lower threshold → nonsignificant ↑ NDI at 18–21 months (OR 1.74; 95% CI 0.98–3.11); significant ใน post-hoc with more inclusive NDI measure (Whyte Pediatrics 2009)

TOP trial (Kirpalani NEJM 2020;383:2639): 1824 infants; higher threshold did NOT improve survival without NDI at 22–26 months (RR 1.00; 95% CI 0.92–1.10); no difference in survival or common morbidities
ETTNO trial (Franz JAMA 2020;324:560): no difference in death or disability at 24 months (risk difference 1.6%; 95% CI −4.8% to 7.9%)

สรุป: ใช้ Lower Hb Threshold ใน Most Circumstances
TOP + ETTNO (2020): higher threshold ไม่ได้ improve outcomes → สนับสนุน lower threshold ใน most clinical circumstances. แต่: thresholds ต่ำกว่าที่ศึกษาใน trials เหล่านี้ = uncertain safety — ห้ามใช้ threshold ต่ำกว่า 7.0 g/dL routinely. สำหรับ surgical patients: Hct ≥40% อาจต้องการ; asymptomatic preterm: อาจ tolerate Hct 21%.
2.3 Iron Deficiency หลัง Conservative Approach
RBC transfusion = source ของ parenteral iron; fewer transfusions → ↑ risk iron-deficiency anemia
Iron deficiency → adverse neurodevelopment (Lozoff NEJM 1991;325:687)
Higher enteral iron → better cognitive outcomes at 2 years (German J Pediatr 2021, PENUT post-hoc)
Implication: conservative transfusion approach ต้องคู่กับ appropriate enteral iron supplementation
2.4 Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents (ESA)
EPO/darbepoetin: modest reduction ใน RBC transfusion + donor exposure (Aher Cochrane 2014)
PENUT trial (Juul NEJM 2020;382:233): multicenter; high-dose EPO did NOT reduce death or severe NDI at 2 years (RR 1.03; 95% CI 0.81–1.32)
Some studies (Ohls Pediatrics 2013/2014): darbepoetin → ↑ cognitive outcomes — need more evidence; ESA ยังไม่ใช่ standard of care สำหรับ neuroprotection
3. Risks of Red Blood Cell Transfusion
3.1 Transfusion-Transmitted Infections

rows: None reported in United States (<1 in 3 million)

blocks.text: Donor screening: ABO/Rh typing, weak D antigen testing; FDA-licensed tests for HIV, HepB, HepC, HTLV I/II, WNV, syphilis, Trypanosoma cruzi

Emerging pathogens: Zika, Ebola, dengue, chikungunya — highlight residual risk; pathogen reduction (psoralen + UV irradiation) approved by FDA for plasma + platelets; ongoing trials for whole blood/RBCs

⚠️ Pathogen Reduction + Phototherapy = Contraindicated
Pathogen reduction ใช้ psoralen (photosensitizer) + UV irradiation. ถ้า neonate ได้รับ phototherapy ด้วย: psoralen additive + phototherapy UV → skin erythema. ดังนั้น ห้ามใช้ pathogen-reduced products ใน infants ที่กำลัง phototherapy. Hepatitis A + E virus: NOT inactivated by pathogen reduction.
3.2 Immunologic Complications
Acute immunologic: hemolytic reaction, immune-mediated platelet destruction, febrile nonhemolytic reaction, allergic reaction, anaphylaxis, TRALI (transfusion-related acute lung injury)
Delayed immunologic: delayed hemolytic reaction, alloimmunization (WBC/RBC/platelet antigens), post-transfusion purpura, TA-GvHD (transfusion-associated graft-vs-host disease)
TRIM (transfusion-related immunomodulation): immunosuppressive effect; studied pre-leukoreduction; not investigated in neonates
TRALI — Transfusion-Related Acute Lung Injury
Most severe acute complication; largely from plasma + platelet components but can occur after RBC
Immune TRALI: HLA + granulocyte-binding alloantibodies; adult incidence: ~1/5000 transfusions; case-fatality 6%–9%; incidence/risks in neonates unclear
Nonimmune TRALI: biologically active lipids from stored products; more benign
Currently TRALI = most frequent cause of acute transfusion fatality — 37% ของ deaths (FDA data); uncertainty if occurs in neonatal patients
TRAGI — Transfusion-Related Acute Gut Injury
Proposed entity: NEC occurring within 48 hours of RBC transfusion
Evidence: some observational studies support association; meta-analysis (Garg J Perinat Med 2018): highly heterogeneous — no overall association
RCT data: no difference in NEC between higher vs lower transfusion thresholds (TOP, ETTNO, PINT)
Alternative hypothesis: anemia (Patel RM JAMA 2016;315:889) หรือ both anemia + transfusion → gut injury; causality remains uncertain
3.3 Nonimmunologic Complications

TACO (transfusion-associated circulatory overload): large-volume (>20mL/kg) transfusion → CHF + pulmonary edema; severe anemia without active bleeding: slow correction; hydrops fetalis → isovolumetric exchange transfusion
Hypothermia: RBCs stored at 4°C → large-volume/massive transfusion → use blood warmer; monitor especially intraoperatively

Metabolite derangements:: citrate (CPDA-1, AS-3) → calcium chelator → hypocalcemia; mannitol (AS-1) → diuresis; hyperkalemia/hypokalemia ใน impaired renal function / massive transfusion; washing RBCs → ↓ hyperkalemia risk

Transfusion-related death: historical leading cause = ABO-incompatible hemolysis; now rare in neonates (no antibodies <4 months); bacterial sepsis from platelet transfusion = 3rd most common cause: Klebsiella, E. coli, Staph epidermidis — all gram-negative predominant
4. Platelet Transfusion
4.1 Components
Platelet concentrates (random-donor): from whole blood; ~7×10¹⁰ platelets/unit; pool 5–8 units = 1 apheresis unit equivalent
Single-donor apheresis platelets: collected by apheresis; 3–6×10¹¹ platelets/unit
Shelf life: 5 days only; stored at room temperature with constant gentle agitation (ต่างจาก RBC ที่ใช้ 4°C)
Dose: 10–15 mL/kg
4.2 Indications & Epidemiology
Thrombocytopenia (platelet <150,000/μL) = definition; nearly always given prophylactically หรือ for severe bleeding
Incidence: thrombocytopenia affects 18%–35% of NICU admissions; ~70% ของ ELBW infants; แต่ platelet transfusion used only 5%–9% of US NICU infants
4.3 RCTs: Platelet Transfusion Thresholds

rows: No significant difference in new ICH (28% vs 26%, P=.73)
INCREASE in death or severe bleeding with higher threshold (OR 1.57; 95% CI 1.06–2.32)
No significant difference in PDA closure (adj HR 1.4; 95% CI 0.57–3.47)

blocks.text:
PLANET-2 (Curley NEJM 2019): Lower Platelet Threshold = SAFER
Liberal threshold (50×10⁹/L) vs restrictive (25×10⁹/L): increased risk of death or severe bleeding with HIGHER threshold (OR 1.57; 95% CI 1.06–2.32). Consistent across ALL subgroups including high- and low-baseline bleeding risk (Fustolo-Gunnink Blood 2019). Clinical implication: ใช้ threshold ≤25×10⁹/L สำหรับ prophylactic platelet transfusion ใน most preterm infants. ข้อยกเว้น: actively bleeding infants — ไม่ใช้ threshold นี้.
Contributors to platelet dysfunction (beyond count): NSAIDs, acidosis, concomitant coagulopathy
Functional assays (closure time with collagen/ADP) อาจ more important กว่า platelet count ใน predicting bleeding risk (Deschmann JAMA Pediatr 2019)
4.4 Risks of Platelet Transfusion
Bacterial sepsis: ~1/100,000 platelet transfusions; ~1/2000–3000 units contain clinically relevant bacteria
Pathogen inactivation: FDA approved for platelets (+ plasma); reduces bacterial contamination risk further
Platelet refractoriness: concern with pathogen-reduced platelets in immature neonates — needs more study
Same immunologic risks as RBC: TRALI, TRIM, alloimmunization
5. Plasma and Cryoprecipitate Transfusion
5.1 Components
Fresh Frozen Plasma (FFP): frozen within 6–8 hours of collection
F24 plasma: frozen within 24 hours; slightly lower factor VIII but comparable to FFP
Coagulation factors in plasma: (1) fibrinogen, (2) factor XIII, (3) vWF, (4) factor VIII (~100ng/mL bound to vWF), (5) vitamin K-dependent factors II (prothrombin), VII, IX, X
Cryoprecipitate: = FFP thawed + refrozen in 10–15mL; high concentration product:
Factor VIII: 80–150 U/unit
vWF: 100–150 U/unit
Fibrinogen: ~250 mg/unit
Factor XIII: 150–250 U/unit
Fibronectin: ~2 mg/mL
Storage: ≤-18°C, up to 1 year
5.2 Indications
Plasma: coagulopathy / DIC; colloid for ↑ intravascular volume (low oncotic pressure or no response to crystalloid)
Wide variation based on prematurity, surgery, comorbidities; ↑ coagulation testing → ↑ plasma transfusion use (Catford Transfusion 2014)
Most common INR triggering plasma transfusion: 1.7 (10th–90th percentile: 1.2–2.8) (Patel J Pediatr 2021)
Cryoprecipitate: acquired hypofibrinogenemia (fibrinogen <100–150 mg/dL); part of massive transfusion protocol
Cryoprecipitate dose: 5–10mL/kg (or ~0.5 units based on weight); full unit (15–20mL) often too large a dose for most infants
5.3 Evidence (หรือ Lack Thereof)
NNNI trial (Elbourne Eur J Pediatr 1996;155:580): prophylactic FFP vs dextrose/gelatin in preterm infants — NO beneficial effect on death or cranial ultrasound abnormality
No evidence that abnormal coag values ใน first week → serious hemorrhage including IVH (Christensen Transfusion 2014)
No contemporary RCTs to guide plasma use for bleeding prevention in neonates

Thromboelastography (TEG) — Tool ที่ดีกว่า INR?
TEG วัด multiple aspects: clot formation, strength, fibrinolysis → more precise guidance for plasma transfusion vs single INR. Sewell Arch Dis Child FN 2017;102:F79: TEG reference values in term neonates. Potential advantage: ลด unnecessary plasma transfusion (and its thrombosis risk). นำมาใช้เพิ่มขึ้นใน NICU แต่ยังขาด RCT evidence ใน neonates.
5.4 Risks of Plasma/Cryoprecipitate
TRALI, infection (same as RBC)
Venous thrombosis: Maruyama Acta Med Okayama 2012: FFP >50mL/kg in first 5 days → ↑ venous thrombosis (adj OR 5.9; 95% CI 1.1–41.8) in ELBW infants
Children: widespread use of FFP in US children's hospitals despite limited evidence (Puetz J Pediatr 2012)
6. Special Circumstances
6.1 Massive Transfusion
Indication: large-volume blood loss; balanced ratios based on adult/pediatric evidence
Suggested ratio: 1:1 RBCs:FFP + alternating platelets and cryoprecipitate (Diab Br J Haematol 2013; Hendrickson Transfusion 2012)
Whole blood in adults (trauma): may be associated with better outcomes (Repine J Trauma 2006); not sufficiently studied in preterm infants
Fresh whole blood for cardiac bypass priming: no advantage over combination products; associated with ↑ LOS + fluid overload (Mou NEJM 2004;351:1635)
Monitor: metabolic complications + TACO during massive transfusion — close monitoring required
6.2 Exchange Transfusion (ET)
Indications: (1) severe hyperbilirubinemia (ABO/Rh incompatibility or minor antigen); (2) anemia with fluid overload (hydrops fetalis); (3) hemochromatosis
Now relatively uncommon: only 5/111,009 infants in California NICUs (Newman Pediatrics 2003;111:1303); ~1.9/100,000 live births modern era (Bhutani J Perinatol 2016)
Why ET works for hyperbilirubinemia: rapidly removes bilirubin + removes/dilutes offending antibody + RBC antigen target
Procedure — Double-Volume ET
Volume: remove 2× estimated circulating blood volume: 70–80mL/kg (term), 90–100mL/kg (preterm); RBCs reconstituted with plasma
Replace ~85% of native circulating RBCs
Aliquots: 5mL/kg or less (absolute 5–20mL); serial exchanges over 1–3 hours
RBCs: must be leukoreduced; irradiated ถ้าเป็น intrauterine transfusion
Risks ของ Exchange Transfusion
Hypocalcemia: 29%–38% (citrate chelates calcium); thrombocytopenia: 38%–44%; infection (Keenan Pediatrics 1985)
Less common: catheter complications, thrombosis, bleeding, hypothermia, NEC, cardiac arrhythmia
Historical mortality: 3/1000 procedures (0.3%); modern-era risk difficult to estimate given infrequent use
Partial ET สำหรับ Polycythemia
Indication: Hct ≥65%–70%; remove whole blood + replace with colloid/crystalloid
Evidence: benefit on long-term neurodevelopment NOT well established (Ozek Cochrane 2010;CD005089)
6.3 Intravenous Immune Globulin (IVIG)
Indication: hemolytic disease refractory to intensive phototherapy; AAP guideline 2004
Mechanism: blocks Fc receptors in splenic RES → reduces antibody-mediated RBC/platelet destruction
Alcock Cochrane 2002 (CD003313): IVIG ↓ risk of ET + ↓ subsequent ET among those receiving ET
Louis Arch Dis Child FN 2014;99:F325: updated meta-analysis — studies with low risk of bias show NO clear benefit of IVIG in isoimmune hemolytic disease
Risks: hypotension (common — reduce infusion rate); rare: viral infection, NEC (Figueras-Aloy Pediatrics 2010); overall well tolerated (Ohlsson Cochrane 2013 — no serious adverse effects)
7. Neonatal Alloimmune Thrombocytopenia (NAIT)
7.1 Pathophysiology
Mechanism: maternal alloimmunization against paternally derived platelet antigens (>27 HPAs known)
HPA-1a (PlA1) most common; mother HPA-1a negative + anti-HPA-1a antibodies; fetus HPA-1a positive (from father) → transplacental IgG → immune-mediated platelet destruction
Incidence: ~1/1000 live births
Mother typically negative for HPA-1a and often (not always) positive for anti-HPA-1a antibodies (Kjeldsen-Kragh Blood 2007)
7.2 Clinical Features
Severe hemorrhage: ~10/100,000 neonates affected with NAIT; importantly hemorrhage frequently occurs BEFORE birth (intrauterine ICH — most severe outcome)
First pregnancy ก็ affected ได้ (แตกต่างจาก Rh alloimmunization) — first-born severe NAIT possible
7.3 Management
Prenatal: IVIG treatment → ↑ fetal platelet counts (Bussel NEJM 1988;319:1374); benefit on reducing intracranial hemorrhage unclear
Future pregnancies: close monitoring + testing of father + fetus if indicated

Postnatal treatment: transfuse with maternal platelets (HPA-1a negative) หรือ random donor platelets; no clear benefit of postnatal IVIG; may use IVIG ถ้า platelets ไม่ available immediately (antibody-mediated clearance)
Thrombocytopenia resolves as passive maternal antibody degrades over time
Guidelines: International Collaboration for Transfusion Medicine Guidelines (ICTMG) (Lieberman Br J Haematol 2019)
8. Decreasing the Need for RBC Transfusion
8.1 Delayed Cord Clamping (DCC) — Most Important
Placental transfusion via DCC: ↓ RBC transfusion need + reduces in-hospital mortality (Fogarty Am J Obstet Gynecol 2018;218:1–18 — systematic review + meta-analysis)
DCC should be part of institutional transfusion protocol for preterm infants

⚠️ Cord Milking: AVOID ใน <28 Weeks
Cord milking (umbilical cord milking toward infant prior to clamping) เพิ่ม risk ของ severe IVH ใน <28 weeks (Katheria JAMA 2019;322:1877). ใช้ DCC แทน ใน extremely preterm infants. Cord milking acceptable in ≥28 weeks ถ้า DCC ไม่สามารถทำได้.
8.2 Minimize Phlebotomy Losses
Phlebotomy = สาเหตุหลักของ anemia of prematurity in ELBW (Rosebraugh Transfusion 2013; Widness Neoreviews 2008)
Strategies: point-of-care testing (micro-sampling), inline/inline blood sampling devices, limit unnecessary lab draws, closed-circuit sampling
Blood conservation techniques: Carroll & Widness Semin Perinatol 2012 — review of nonpharmacological strategies
8.3 Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents
Modest effect on reducing RBC transfusion (Aher Cochrane 2014 for early + late EPO; Ohlsson Cochrane 2014)
PENUT trial (Juul NEJM 2020): high-dose EPO → no reduction in death/severe NDI at 2 years — major trial does not support routine EPO for neuroprotection
Some neurocognitive benefit: darbepoetin in small trials (Ohls Pediatrics 2013/2014) — needs replication
9. NICU Practical — Transfusion Decisions วันต่อวัน
RBC threshold decision: ใช้ restrictive (lower) threshold ใน most cases; check respiratory status + postnatal age + clinical condition; document indication และ pretransfusion Hb every time
Platelet transfusion: prophylactic threshold: ≤25×10⁹/L ใน most stable preterm infants (PLANET-2 evidence); actively bleeding: higher threshold acceptable; measure functional assay ถ้า available

FFP / cryoprecipitate: avoid prophylactic plasma for IVH prevention (no evidence); ใช้ เมื่อ coagulopathy + active bleeding; consider TEG ถ้า available; cryoprecipitate ใน hypofibrinogenemia (<100–150mg/dL)

Exchange transfusion (ET): rare now; indications: bilirubin ที่ ET threshold (AAP 2022 guidelines), Rh/ABO hemolytic disease refractory to IVIG + intensive phototherapy, hydrops; use CPDA-1 (ไม่ใช่ AS-1/AS-3); irradiate ถ้า intrauterine; monitor Ca²⁺ + platelets ทุก 30–60 min during procedure

NAIT workup: thrombocytopenia ใน healthy term infant ที่ไม่มี explanation → consider NAIT; test mother + father for HPA typing; maternal platelets (washed, irradiated) = preferred; IVIG ถ้า platelets not available; อย่ารอ test result ถ้า severe
IVIG ใน hemolytic disease: bilirubin approaching ET threshold + + HDFN → IVIG (0.5–1 g/kg over 2–4 hr); monitor for hypotension; reduce infusion rate ถ้า hypotension occurs
CMV prevention: ใช้ leukoreduced CMV-negative หรือ leukoreduced CMV-untested (ไม่ delay transfusion); อย่า delay RBC ถ้า CMV-negative unavailable
Storage solution check: ก่อน large-volume (≥20mL/kg) หรือ ET: verify CPDA-1 unit (ไม่ใช่ AS-1/AS-3); หากต้องใช้ AS: wash RBCs
DCC protocol: ทุก preterm delivery: delay cord clamping ≥30–60 seconds ถ้า stable; ห้าม cord milk ใน <28 weeks
Iron supplementation: conservative transfusion approach → start enteral iron เร็ว; track iron stores (ferritin); iron deficiency = neurodevelopmental risk
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — PLANET-2: ให้ Platelet น้อยลงไม่ใช่มากขึ้น
Platelet threshold 50 (liberal) เพิ่ม death/severe bleeding vs threshold 25 (restrictive) — counterintuitive แต่ confirmed RCT. Prophylactic platelet threshold ≤25×10⁹/L ใน stable preterm. Exception: actively bleeding infant = individual clinical judgment. อย่า transfuse platelet เพราะ 'กลัว' เลข — treat the patient.

PEARL 2 — AS-1/AS-3 ≥20mL/kg: ห้ามใช้
AS-1 มี mannitol (diuresis), AS-3 มี adenine (renal toxicity). Large-volume (≥20mL/kg) transfusion = CPDA-1 units only. Exchange transfusion, massive transfusion: ตรวจ label storage solution ก่อนทุกครั้ง. CPDA-1 shelf life 35 วัน vs AS 42 วัน — อาจต้องเตรียมล่วงหน้า.

PEARL 3 — Cord Milking <28 Weeks = IVH Risk
DCC ลด RBC transfusion + ลด mortality (Fogarty 2018). Cord milking เพิ่ม severe IVH ใน <28 weeks (Katheria JAMA 2019). ดังนั้น ใน <28 weeks: DCC เท่านั้น, ห้าม cord milk. ใน ≥28 weeks: cord milking อาจใช้ได้ถ้า DCC ไม่สามารถทำได้.

PEARL 4 — ET: Monitor Ca²⁺ + Platelets
Exchange transfusion: citrate chelates Ca²⁺ → hypocalcemia 29%–38%; thrombocytopenia 38%–44%. Monitor iCa²⁺ ทุก 30–60 min; ให้ 10% Ca gluconate ถ้า iCa²⁺ ลด; platelet count หลัง ET ก่อน assume platelet normal. Run ≤4 hours; 5mL/kg aliquots; use blood warmer.

PEARL 5 — Prophylactic FFP ไม่ลด IVH
NNNI trial (Elbourne 1996): prophylactic FFP vs dextrose/gelatin — ไม่มี benefit ต่อ death หรือ cranial abnormality. ยิ่งกว่านั้น: FFP >50mL/kg ใน 5 วันแรก → ↑ venous thrombosis (OR 5.9) (Maruyama 2012). ห้าม transfuse FFP เพื่อ 'prevent IVH' — ไม่มีหลักฐาน + อาจเป็นอันตราย.
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Kirpalani H et al. NEJM 2020;383:2639 (TOP trial): 1824 preterm infants; higher vs lower Hb threshold — no improvement in survival without NDI at 22–26 months (RR 1.00; 95% CI 0.92–1.10); supports conservative approach.
Franz AR et al. JAMA 2020;324:560 (ETTNO trial): Liberal vs restrictive thresholds in ELBW — no difference in death or disability at 24 months (risk difference 1.6%; 95% CI −4.8%–7.9%).

Curley A et al. NEJM 2019;380:242 (PLANET-2): Liberal platelet threshold (50×10⁹/L) vs restrictive (25×10⁹/L) — increased death or severe bleeding with liberal threshold (OR 1.57; 95% CI 1.06–2.32); landmark trial changing practice.
Fustolo-Gunnink SF et al. Blood 2019;134:2354: PLANET-2 subgroup analysis — preterm neonates benefit from low platelet threshold regardless of baseline bleeding/mortality risk; findings robust.
Juul SE et al. NEJM 2020;382:233 (PENUT trial): High-dose EPO did not reduce death or severe NDI at 2 years in extremely preterm infants (RR 1.03; 95% CI 0.81–1.32).
Katheria A et al. JAMA 2019;322:1877: Cord milking vs delayed cord clamping — cord milking associated with higher rate of death or severe IVH in <28 weeks infants; DCC preferred.
Fogarty M et al. Am J Obstet Gynecol 2018;218:1–18: Systematic review and meta-analysis of delayed vs early cord clamping in preterm infants — DCC reduces RBC transfusion and in-hospital mortality.

Fergusson DA et al. JAMA 2012;308:1443 (ARIPI trial): Fresh (mean 5 days) vs standard (mean 15 days) RBC in VLBW — no difference in composite mortality/morbidity; storage age alone does not determine outcome.
Josephson CD et al. JAMA Pediatr 2014;168:1054: CMV-seronegative leukoreduced RBC in VLBW — TT-CMV risk 0% (95% CI 0%–0.3%) per unit; prospective cohort.

Patel RM et al. J Pediatr 2021;235:92–99 (Variation in Neonatal Transfusion Practice): Wide variation in Hb and platelet thresholds, transfusion volumes, and product selection across US NICUs; highlights patient blood management opportunity.

Patel RM et al. JAMA Netw Open 2021;4:e2123942: Blood donor sex and age — transfusions from older female donors associated with lowest risk of adverse short-term outcomes in VLBW; needs more study before donor selection recommendation.

Elbourne D (NNNI trial). Eur J Pediatr 1996;155:580: Prophylactic FFP vs dextrose or gelatin in preterm infants — no beneficial effect on early mortality or morbidity; first RCT showing no benefit of prophylactic plasma.
Maruyama H et al. Acta Med Okayama 2012;66:61: FFP >50mL/kg in first 5 days in ELBW — associated with increased venous thrombosis (adj OR 5.9; 95% CI 1.1–41.8); caution against liberal FFP use.
Alcock GS, Liley H. Cochrane 2002 (CD003313): IVIG infusion for isoimmune haemolytic jaundice — reduces need for ET and subsequent ET; classic systematic review supporting IVIG use.
Louis D et al. Arch Dis Child FN 2014;99:F325: Updated meta-analysis of IVIG in HDFN — studies with low risk of bias show no clear benefit; challenges prior conclusions.
Busch MP et al. Blood 2019;133:1854: Comprehensive review of prevention of transfusion-transmitted infections — current risk estimates for HIV, HepB/C, CMV, WNV, etc. in US.

Deschmann E et al. JAMA Pediatr 2019;173:393: In vitro bleeding time correlates with bleeding in preterm thrombocytopenic infants; functional assays may be superior to platelet count alone for bleeding risk assessment.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Preterm infant GA 26 weeks อายุ 5 วัน ใน low respiratory support (CPAP 5 cmH₂O) มี Hb 8.5 g/dL — ควร transfuse ไหม? อ้างอิง threshold อะไร?

ตอบ: Hb 8.5 g/dL ใน low respiratory support = conservative/restrictive approach: ตาม TOP (NEJM 2020) + ETTNO (JAMA 2020): lower threshold appropriate ส่วนใหญ่. Restrictive thresholds ใน these trials: ~7.0–7.5 g/dL (lower limit for ventilated/high support) หรือ ~8.5–9.3 g/dL (upper). Hb 8.5 g/dL ใน stable CPAP อาจไม่ต้อง transfuse ถ้า clinical picture stable — ดู symptoms (tachycardia, apnea, poor weight gain, ↑ FiO₂). อย่า transfuse ที่ threshold ต่ำกว่า 7.0 g/dL เพราะ safety data ไม่มี. Document indication + pretransfusion Hb ทุกครั้ง.
Q: PLANET-2 trial (Curley NEJM 2019) พบอะไร? clinical implication ต่อ platelet transfusion?

ตอบ: PLANET-2: randomized preterm infants to prophylactic platelet transfusion threshold 50×10⁹/L (liberal) vs 25×10⁹/L (restrictive). ผล: death or severe bleeding INCREASED with liberal threshold (OR 1.57; 95% CI 1.06–2.32). Consistent across all subgroups (Fustolo-Gunnink Blood 2019). Clinical implication: prophylactic platelet transfusion threshold ≤25×10⁹/L for most stable preterm infants. ข้อยกเว้น: actively bleeding infants — does not apply; clinical judgment required.
Q: Why should AS-1 or AS-3 units NOT be used for large-volume (≥20mL/kg) transfusion ใน neonates?

ตอบ: AS-1 contains mannitol — osmotic diuretic; AS-3 contains adenine — associated with renal tubular toxicity. Large-volume transfusion → deliver significant doses of these additives → diuresis + renal toxicity. ดังนั้น use CPDA-1 units for large-volume or exchange transfusion. CPDA-1 shelf life 35 days (vs 42 for AS); ต้อง plan ahead. ถ้า must use AS: wash RBCs before large-volume transfusion.
Q: Risks หลักของ exchange transfusion ใน neonate มีอะไรบ้าง? จะป้องกัน hypocalcemia ได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Common risks: hypocalcemia 29%–38%, thrombocytopenia 38%–44%, infection. Less common: catheter complications, thrombosis, bleeding, hypothermia, NEC, arrhythmia. Historical mortality: 0.3% (3/1000 procedures). Prevention of hypocalcemia: citrate (CPDA-1 + AS-3) chelates free Ca²⁺ → monitor iCa²⁺ ทุก 30–60 min during procedure; give 10% calcium gluconate IV slowly ถ้า iCa²⁺ ลด หรือ prophylactically per protocol. ยัง monitor platelets หลัง ET ก่อนสมมติว่า platelet count stable.
Q: IVIG ใน hemolytic disease of the newborn — mechanism, evidence, และ คำเตือน?

ตอบ: Mechanism: blocks Fc receptors in splenic RES → reduces antibody-mediated destruction of RBCs (coated by maternal IgG antibodies). Evidence: Alcock Cochrane 2002 (CD003313): IVIG ↓ risk of ET + subsequent ET. Louis Arch Dis Child FN 2014: updated meta-analysis — studies with low risk of bias show no clear benefit. แปลว่า evidence ยังขัดแย้ง. Caution: hypotension (common) — reduce infusion rate; rare: NEC (Figueras-Aloy Pediatrics 2010), viral infection.
Q: Pathogen reduction technology ทำงานอย่างไร? มี contraindication อะไรใน NICU?

ตอบ: Mechanism: UV irradiation + psoralen (photosensitizer) → damage pathogen nucleic acids → prevent replication + infection. FDA approved for plasma + platelets; ongoing trials for RBCs. Inactivates: broad range of viruses, bacteria, parasites. Does NOT inactivate: hepatitis A + hepatitis E virus. Contraindication: infants receiving phototherapy — psoralen additive + phototherapy UV → skin erythema.
Q: NAIT คืออะไร? ทำไมต่างจาก Rh alloimmunization? management หลังคลอด?

ตอบ: NAIT: maternal alloimmunization ต่อ paternally-derived platelet antigens (HPA-1a most common, >27 HPAs); ~1/1000 live births. ต่างจาก Rh: first pregnancy ก็ affected ได้ (Rh = second pregnancy); severe ICH อาจเกิด in utero. Management: transfuse maternal platelets (HPA-1a negative, washed, irradiated) หรือ random donor platelets; IVIG ถ้า platelets not available; thrombocytopenia resolves as maternal antibody degrades. Future pregnancies: close monitoring, test father + fetus.
Q: Cord milking vs delayed cord clamping ใน preterm — evidence สำคัญอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: DCC (Fogarty Am J Obstet Gynecol 2018): ↓ RBC transfusion + ↓ in-hospital mortality ใน preterm infants — landmark meta-analysis. Cord milking ใน <28 weeks (Katheria JAMA 2019;322:1877): associated with ↑ death or severe IVH vs DCC. Conclusion: <28 weeks = DCC only; avoid cord milking. ≥28 weeks: cord milking may be acceptable if DCC unavailable. DCC should be institutionalized as part of transfusion reduction strategy.
Q: Thromboelastography (TEG) — ข้อดีเหนือ INR ใน guiding plasma transfusion?

ตอบ: INR/PT วัดแค่ factor activation in vitro — ไม่สะท้อน in vivo hemostasis ครบ. TEG วัด: clot initiation, formation rate, clot strength, fibrinolysis — holistic picture. ประโยชน์ใน NICU: ระบุ specific hemostatic defect (factor deficiency vs platelet dysfunction vs hyperfibrinolysis) → targeted transfusion (FFP vs cryo vs platelets) แทน empirical approach. Sewell Arch Dis Child FN 2017: TEG reference values in term neonates. ยังขาด RCT evidence ใน NICU แต่ concept sound.
Q: TOP trial (NEJM 2020) vs PINT trial (J Pediatr 2006) — ผลต่างกันอย่างไร? implication?

ตอบ: PINT (Kirpalani 2006): lower threshold → nonsignificant trend toward ↑ NDI (OR 1.74; CI 0.98–3.11); significant ใน post-hoc. กังวลว่า restrictive อาจ harm. TOP (Kirpalani 2020): 1824 infants (more powered); higher threshold did NOT improve survival without NDI (RR 1.00; CI 0.92–1.10). ETTNO 2020: concordant. Implication: earlier concerns from PINT not confirmed in larger trials; lower threshold is safe and appropriate for most preterm. Safety below studied thresholds (~7.0 g/dL) remains unknown.
Day 71 Preview

Avery Chapter 71 — Neonatal Leukocyte Physiology and Disorders
พรุ่งนี้: Neonatal Leukocyte Physiology and Disorders — จาก blood transfusion (Day 70) ขยับไปที่ white blood cells: leukocyte development + granulopoiesis, neonatal neutrophil function (impaired chemotaxis, ↓ reserve), neonatal leukemoid reactions (trisomy 21 + transient myeloproliferative disorder), congenital neutropenias, leukocyte adhesion deficiency (LAD), G-CSF / GM-CSF ใน neonatal sepsis — evidence และ current guideline.
